phases.name: Global Intelligence Directive

steps.name: BigQuery schema migration for intel tables
Institutional extractors (GS, BLK, Bridgewater, JPM)
steps.dropped_reason: Public institutional pieces are paywalled, lagged, PR-filtered; scraping carries ToS/compliance risk. Positional signal institutional research telegraphs is better captured by 13F holdings.

steps.name: Academic extractors (arXiv OAI-PMH, SSRN, MIT, Stanford, Oxford)
steps.dropped_reason: McLean & Pontiff 2016 (replicated 2025): published quant-fin strategies lose 50-60% of edge post-publication. arXiv/SSRN monitoring is a factor graveyard, not an alpha pipeline.

steps.name: AI-frontier extractors (Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepMind, Google Research)
steps.dropped_reason: AI-frontier blogs inform model/harness choice (reduces compute burn) but not trading decisions. Out-of-loop for alpha per the phase goal. Belongs in a model-upgrades or MCP-hygiene phase.

steps.name: Player-driven extractors (SeekingAlpha, WSB daily, QuantConnect, SWF EDGAR)
steps.dropped_reason: Springer Digital Finance 2023 + ScienceDirect 2024: WSB high-attention positions average -8.5% holding-period return; Reddit sentiment Sharpe is ~50% of market. Negative long-term alpha in peer-reviewed record. Phase-7.5 (Reddit WSB) + phase-7.2 (13F) already capture what is worth keeping.

steps.name: Novelty client (Voyage + Gemini fallback) and prompt-patch queue
Slack digest + daily scheduler wiring
steps.dropped_reason: A daily digest over a 4-table intel schema is noise without signal-bearing extractors. The 6.5.9 smoketest already proves the schema is queryable. Defer dashboard/surfacing work until after phase-8.5 demonstrates intel actually flows into strategy candidates.

phases.goal: Turn pyfinagent into a continuously-learning intelligence engine by ingesting high-signal external reports (institutional, academic, AI-frontier, player-driven) into BigQuery, scoring novelty, and surfacing actionable prompt-patches and a daily Slack digest. Hard boundary: ingest + score + surface only; no automatic capital allocation changes.

path_decision.summary: Keep 4 of 9 steps (6.5.1 schema, 6.5.2 registry, 6.5.7 prompt-patch queue, 6.5.9 smoketest). Drop 5 source-specific extractors + digest, with pointers to phase-7 / phase-8.5 where the work is better located.
path_decision.open_issue: 6.5.7 prompt-patch queue has no source-specific extractor feeding it after drops. Before 6.5.7 ships, its contract MUST either (a) wire one or more phase-7 feature outputs into intel_prompt_patches, or (b) explicitly accept empty-pipe at launch and gate closure on a follow-up phase-7-integration step.

congress.py upsert_trades docstring says MERGE but implements stream-insert; cleanup pass when shared HTTP infra lands.
f13.py smoketested Berkshire only. Scheduler fan-out to top-N filers lives here.

House chamber data not yet ingested (S3 bucket 403; GitHub mirror Senate-only). Parse disclosures-clerk.house.gov PDF/XML ZIP before relying on full-Congress signal.
alt_13f_holdings.ticker is NULL. Resolve CUSIP->ticker here via an existing mapping service.
Signal extraction from alt_finra_short_volume must block until owner risk-accept OR docs/compliance/alt-data.md row 7.3 amendment.

steps.blocker: Immutable verification command references .claude/agents/qa-evaluator.md and harness-verifier.md; these were intentionally merged into qa.md by phase-4.15.0 (PREP: MAS restructure to 3 agents). CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids re-splitting them: "Re-split agents: reintroducing Explore or harness-verifier as separate files after they have been merged is the old pattern." Verification text would need to be updated to target .claude/agents/qa.md + researcher.md instead, but verification criteria are immutable. Needs owner decision: amend criterion OR retire the step.

phases.name: Harness Discipline Patches (research-gate ≥5, handoff layout, docs)

steps.name: Upgrade researcher.md: require >=5 sources read in full + last-2yr scan
Main/Orchestrator agent individual behavior
steps.description: Run a minimal harness cycle and verify the orchestrator completes one plan->generate->evaluate cycle, writes all five handoff artifacts, and appends one harness_log entry. Isolates Main's file-management and sub-agent dispatch from sub-agent correctness.

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python scripts/harness/run_harness.py --dry-run --cycles 1 --iterations-per-cycle 1 2>&1 | tee /tmp/harness_dry_run.log && grep -E 'Cycle [0-9]+' handoff/harness_log.md | tail -1 && ls handoff/current/contract.md handoff/current/experiment_results.md handoff/current/evaluator_critique.md

steps.name: Researcher agent individual behavior
steps.description: Spawn the researcher with a canned simple-tier prompt. Verify the brief contains the JSON envelope with gate_passed=true and external_sources_read_in_full>=5. Verifies tool access (WebFetch/WebSearch), envelope discipline, and the 5-source floor.

steps.name: Q/A agent individual behavior
steps.description: Spawn Q/A against a known-good experiment_results fixture with one deliberate gap. Assert verdict JSON contains ok/verdict/checks_run keys, anti-rubber-stamp logic surfaces the gap as CONDITIONAL rather than PASS, and the 5-item harness-compliance audit is present.

steps.name: Inter-agent handoff integrity
steps.description: Run one full (non-dry) harness cycle on a minimal real step. Verify all five handoff artifacts exist and are non-empty, harness_log is appended, and masterplan status flip is recorded. Canonical file-based communication gate per Anthropic harness design doc.

steps.name: CoALA memory layers (working / episodic / semantic / procedural)
steps.description: Probe each of the 4 CoALA memory layers independently: working=orchestrator import, episodic=LearningLogger can write to BQ harness_learning_log, semantic=AgentMemory BM25 loads, procedural=every backend/agents/skills/*.md is readable and non-empty. Per-layer pass/fail; no aggregation.

steps.name: End-to-end signal generation with evidence traceability
steps.description: POST /api/analyze for AAPL, poll to completion, verify signals API returns AAPL with a non-empty sources/evidence field. Tests the 28-agent orchestrator pipeline at integration level without a browser.

steps.name: Paper trading execution against virtual portfolio
steps.description: Submit a paper BUY order (AAPL x 1) via /api/paper-trading/order, verify the order appears in /api/paper-trading/orders within 60s, and confirm the BQ paper_trades table gains a row. Virtual portfolio only; no real capital.

steps.name: Slack interface startup + command registration
steps.description: Start backend/slack_bot/app.py as a subprocess, wait for Socket Mode connected log line or equivalent, verify command handlers register without import error, then terminate. Dry-run (process alive + log line) acceptable when Slack tokens are absent in the test env.

steps.name: Self-update deploy system audit
steps.description: Static + dry-run audit of backend/slack_bot/self_update.py: syntax clean, logs/ dir writable, `git fetch --dry-run` reachable, no hardcoded absolute paths outside project root. Does NOT execute a real git pull or restart.

steps.name: Aggregate gate -- full go_live_drills suite + critical-incident check
steps.description: Run pytest on scripts/go_live_drills/ (includes 4.17.1-4.17.9 + 23 pre-existing drills). Assert zero failures and zero CRITICAL / HARNESS HALT entries in the last 50 lines of harness_log.md. When this step passes, the old phase-4 step 4.9 (monolithic aggregate.sh) is superseded and retired.

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest scripts/go_live_drills/ -v --tb=short -q 2>&1 | tee /tmp/go_live_drills.log && python scripts/go_live_drills/aggregate_gate_check.py

steps.name: OpenClaw runtime on Mac Mini -- cron/launchd health
steps.description: Verify the launchd plist / cron entry that triggers the pyfinagent harness on Peder's Mac Mini is loaded, points at the correct venv + working directory, and successfully invokes run_harness.py on its scheduled cadence. Inspect the last N launchd invocations via `launchctl list | grep pyfinagent` and `log show --predicate 'subsystem contains "pyfinagent"' --last 2h`. Confirms the OpenClaw runtime surface that spawns this Claude Code session cron-style.

steps.name: F1 failure-discipline recovery drill (planted-fault injection)
steps.description: Inject a deliberate planted fault into a smoke harness cycle (e.g., Q/A returns FAIL three consecutive times on identical evidence) and verify the F1 failure-discipline machinery fires: consecutive_fails increments to 3, certified_fallback is raised, revert-not-restart is honored (no duplicate file overwrites), and the incident is logged to handoff/harness_log.md with a CRITICAL marker. Actively exercises the error/logging/recovery path rather than passively tailing the log.

phases.name: Vertex AI generative-models SDK migration
phases.description: Google deprecated the Vertex AI `vertexai.generative_models` SDK on 2025-06-24 and will remove it on 2026-06-24. Migrate to the replacement `google-genai` SDK (`from google import genai`) per https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/deprecations/genai-vertexai-sdk. Our test suite surfaces the deprecation warning on every evaluator_agent import (vertexai/generative_models/_generative_models.py:433). User explicitly requested this be added as the final masterplan step on 2026-04-19.

steps.name: Audit vertexai.generative_models call sites + migration plan

verification.command: python -c "import ast, sys; tree=ast.parse(open('docs/VERTEX_AI_GENAI_MIGRATION.md').read()) if False else None; assert __import__('pathlib').Path('docs/VERTEX_AI_GENAI_MIGRATION.md').exists(), 'migration plan doc missing'; print('ok')"
source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "from google import genai; print('google-genai importable')"
success_criteria: shim module exposes a single import surface

steps.name: Migrate evaluator_agent + orchestrator call sites to google-genai

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && pytest backend/tests/test_evaluator_agent.py -q && python -W error::DeprecationWarning -c "from backend.agents import evaluator_agent"
success_criteria: no DeprecationWarning from vertexai on evaluator import

verification.command: grep -rn 'vertexai.generative_models\|from vertexai import generative_models' backend/ --include=*.py | grep -v test_ | grep -v migration | wc -l | awk '{exit ($1>0)}'

steps.name: Remove vertexai dep + CI warning filter for the deprecation

success_criteria: google-genai is the only Google GenAI SDK pinned

phases.name: Rainbow deploys — safe-update pattern
phases.description: Implement Brandon Dimcheff's Rainbow Deploys pattern (https://brandon.dimcheff.com/2018/02/rainbow-deploys-with-kubernetes/) so dependency bumps, model-version flips, and framework migrations can be rolled out behind labeled colored Deployments/Services and rolled back by flipping a selector. Extends classic blue/green to N simultaneous versions (red/orange/yellow/green/blue/indigo/violet) so the harness can run a canary on ~5% traffic, compare live SLOs against the active color, and promote/demote without a rolling restart. User-requested on 2026-04-19 as future-proofing for the Vertex->google-genai migration (phase-11) and any subsequent SDK bumps.

steps.name: Audit current deploy surface + pick Rainbow scope boundary

verification.command: test -f docs/RAINBOW_DEPLOY_PLAN.md && python -c "import pathlib; p=pathlib.Path('docs/RAINBOW_DEPLOY_PLAN.md'); assert p.stat().st_size > 2000, 'plan doc too thin'; print('ok')"
success_criteria: docs/RAINBOW_DEPLOY_PLAN.md exists with: current deploy surface, scope (backend / frontend / MCP / harness), color palette decision, rollback SLO
deploy/rainbow/ contains at least 2 color deployments + 1 service
kubectl apply --dry-run=client passes on each yaml

steps.name: promote.py + rollback.py CLI for color flip

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python scripts/deploy/rainbow/promote.py --dry-run --to green && python scripts/deploy/rainbow/rollback.py --dry-run
success_criteria: each prints the exact kubectl/service selector patch it would apply
smoke-test against a kind cluster optional (document if skipped)
95/5 traffic split surface (weighted Service or Gateway API HTTPRoute)
canary SLO diff script compares the two colors over a rolling window
test_rainbow_canary.py covers the split + diff logic

steps.name: First Rainbow migration — candidate TBD after 12.3 (was Vertex; Vertex shipped outside Rainbow in phase-11)

success_criteria: phase-11 migration documented to use the Rainbow pattern for the final cutover
dual-write plane parallel to the Service switch
runbook step 'how to roll back' lives in docs/

steps.scope_reassignment_note: Phase-11 Vertex migration shipped directly without Rainbow (2026-04-19). Phase-12.4 will select a replacement migration candidate after phase-12.3 lands; candidates include next Anthropic SDK bump or a dummy color-flip-only rehearsal.

verification.command: python -c "import json; s=json.load(open('.claude/settings.json')); assert s['permissions']['defaultMode'] != 'bypassPermissions'; assert s.get('sandbox',{}).get('enabled') is True"

steps.blocker: User requires bypassPermissions for continuous autonomous-harness operation; acceptEdits + sandbox interrupts workflow. Revisit when harness supports non-interactive acceptEdits or when running in container/VM.

phases.name: MCP config hygiene & operator UX
phases.description: Cleanup of `.mcp.json` / `.claude/settings.local.json` so Claude Code session start is silent when optional credential-bearing MCP servers (e.g., alpaca) are declared but the operator hasn't set the creds. Pattern (per claude-code-guide consult 2026-04-19): use POSIX shell defaults `${VAR:-}` in .mcp.json env blocks to allow empty resolution, + `disabledMcpjsonServers` in per-operator settings.local.json to skip launch AND suppress env-var validation warnings.

steps.name: Silence alpaca missing-env-var warning in .mcp.json

success_criteria: alpaca env block uses ${VAR:-} shell defaults
operator session start no longer warns on missing ALPACA_* env vars

steps.post_commit_note: No harness cycle -- this was a direct fix surfaced at session-start warning. Operator-local .claude/settings.local.json was also updated in-place with `disabledMcpjsonServers: [alpaca]` for belt-and-suspenders (gitignored; per-operator). Pattern documented in this phase's description so future optional-cred MCP servers can follow the same recipe.

phases.name: Multi-Market Expansion (15-step)

path_decision.summary: Replace 3 placeholder steps with 14 concrete implementation sub-steps. Cross-cutting abstractions first (5.1-5.4), then market rollouts (5.6 options, 5.7 FX, 5.8 futures, 5.9 international equities), ETF expansion (5.10), intelligence layer (5.11-5.12), multi-asset backtest (5.13), autonomous loop integration (5.14), integration gate (5.15). Crypto market integration (formerly 5.5) REMOVED per owner directive 2026-04-19. Phase remains at the end of the masterplan.

open_issues.note: EODHD API key ($19.99/mo) needed for 5.2/5.9/5.13. Owner budget approval required.
5.8 (futures) requires IBKR TWS or IB Gateway running locally or on VPS. Owner decides: local dev, VPS, or defer futures until cloud infra stabilizes.
Research recommends: crypto -> options -> FX -> futures -> international. Owner may reorder; each market step is independent once 5.1-5.4 are done.
CFTC registration may be needed before live-capital futures/FX. Paper-trading only for phase-5; live capital gated on owner + counsel review.

_comments: Dependency DAG: 5.1 + 5.4 are zero-dep. 5.15 is the sink. Run 5.1/5.4 first; 5.2/5.3 can overlap. Then 5.6-5.10 per market. Then intelligence 5.11-5.13. Then 5.14 integration. Then 5.15 gate.
Crypto market integration (formerly step 5.5) was dropped on 2026-04-19 per owner directive. See phase-5.archived_dropped_steps for the original record.

Step ids are NOT renumbered after the 5.5 removal so existing references (e.g. Q/A critiques, handoff log) remain valid. Step ids remain 5.1, 5.2, 5.3, 5.4, 5.6, 5.7, 5.8, 5.9, 5.10, 5.11, 5.12, 5.13, 5.14, 5.15 (14 total).
